accurate.why: All claims track the bill text and the statutory context (War Powers Resolution, 50 U.S.C. 1546a, ISAAECA) is correctly stated.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; all findings and provisions match the bill, and contextual statutory references are accurate.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose: directing removal of U.S. Armed Forces from unauthorized hostilities with Iran.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly invokes the War Powers Resolution and the expedited-procedure statutes the resolution relies on.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures the unless-authorized condition and the rule-of-construction carve-outs (self-defense, intelligence, partner assistance, evacuation).

effective_dates.why: Notes the key dates from the findings, including the February 28, 2026 operation and April 8, 2026 ceasefire.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers the operative removal directive, the findings, and the rule of construction faithfully.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive tone with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized by findings, operative provision, and rule of construction; internally consistent.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the IRGC, Israel, the statutes, and the President's directive consistent with the bill.

correct_figures.why: Dates stated (February 28, 2026; April 8, 2026) match the bill; no figures are misstated.
